en.title: Why do dogs spin before lying down? The surprising reason has nothing to do with comfort but ancient survival instincts
en.description: Dogs spin in circles before lying down for a reason, not by accident. Animal behavior experts say this common dog habit comes from wild ancestors that flattened grass, checked for predators, and chose safe sleeping spots
en.title_es: ¿Por qué los perros giran antes de acostarse? La sorprendente razón no tiene nada que ver con la comodidad, sino con los antiguos instintos de supervivencia
en.description_es: Los perros giran en círculos antes de acostarse por una razón, no por accidente. Los expertos en comportamiento animal dicen que este hábito común de los perros proviene de antepasados salvajes que aplanaron el pasto, revisaron a los depredadores y eligieron lugares seguros para dormir

en.title: In 2014, a neuroscientist named Gregory Berns trained a group of dogs to lie completely still inside a functional MRI scanner and measured what happened in their brains when they smelled their owners — and the reward centers of the dogs’ brains lit up more brightly for their owners than for any other scent, including the scent of other dogs in their own households
